text.title: Container Image Digest Pinning Policy Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the image-digest-pinning-policy methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: Production Kubernetes manifests and Helm values MUST pin container images by sha256 digest, never by floating tag.
rule.rationale: Floating tags allow upstream re-tag to inject new code into running clusters; digests are immutable.

rule.statement: Pin only images from the org-approved registry allow-list; mirror upstream images into a controlled registry before pinning.
rule.rationale: Pinning to an unmirror'ed external registry creates a single-vendor uptime dependency for the cluster.

rule.statement: Each image class has a documented maximum-age (e.g. 30 days for base, 7 days for CI runners) and a rotation owner.
rule.rationale: Pinning without rotation is the documented foot-gun — old digests stop receiving CVE fixes.

rule.statement: Any unpinned tag in production lives in an exception register with an owner, expiry date and remediation plan.
rule.rationale: Exceptions exist; an undocumented exception is indistinguishable from a violation.

rule.statement: Cluster admission (Kyverno / OPA / built-in policy) rejects pods whose images are not digest-pinned in the relevant namespaces.
rule.rationale: Policy-as-code is the only durable enforcement; reviewer discipline alone has measurable slippage.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
